The Practice

Asana as a
path of inquiry

Asana
Pranayama
Sadhana
Svadhyaya

The ancient yogis did not practice to perfect a pose — they practiced to know themselves. Every breath, every movement, every moment of stillness is an act of inquiry rooted in the living wisdom of India's yogic heritage.

My classes draw from classical asana, pranayama, and the philosophical texts that have always been yoga's true foundation — the Yoga Sutras, the Bhagavad Gita, and the Upanishads.

Explore classes →
About Swati

A seeker,
a student,
a teacher

I came to yoga not through a studio but through a lineage — a tradition passed through culture, language, and story. My practice is inseparable from the philosophy and heritage from which it grew.

As a teacher and guest lecturer, I bring the full depth of yoga's Indian roots to every class, teacher training, and workshop I lead — making the ancient accessible, alive, and personal.

Work with me →
Offerings

Three ways to study

01
Yoga Classes

Classes rooted in Indian culture and heritage — where asana, pranayama, and philosophy are taught as one inseparable practice.

Asana & Pranayama
Yoga Philosophy
Indian Heritage
02
Guest Lectures

Available for yoga teacher training programs and workshops covering yoga philosophy, history, Sanskrit, and anatomy.

Yoga Philosophy & History
Sanskrit
Anatomy
03
Retreat Instruction

Available for guest instruction and education at retreats — bringing depth, context, and cultural authenticity to immersive experiences.

Guest Instruction
Immersive Education
Retreat Programs
Retreats & Events

Practice in
extraordinary places

Some lessons can only be learned when the ordinary falls away. I bring yoga's philosophical depth to retreat settings — leading classes, workshops, and immersive sessions in places that call the soul inward.

Inquire about retreats →
"Yoga is the journey of the self, through the self, to the self."
— The Bhagavad Gita
